name: Workday

auth.notes: OAuth client is registered in Workday admin (Register API Client). Token and authorize endpoints are tenant-specific and found in the View API Clients tab. Non-expiring refresh tokens can be enabled. An Integration System User (ISU) must be created with appropriate domain security permissions (e.g., Worker Data, Staffing, Organization Information).

endpoints.suspendUser: Managed via business process (Leave of Absence)
endpoints.addToGroup: Managed via business process (Change Organization Assignment)
endpoints.removeFromGroup: Managed via business process (Change Organization Assignment)

api.rateLimits: Not publicly documented. One known limit: 5 requests/second for Strategic Sourcing Projects API. Workday does not publish comprehensive rate limits.

scim.notes: SCIM 2.0 support available for user provisioning. Also used by Workday Strategic Sourcing (separate SCIM endpoint).

soapApi.notes: Many HR operations (hire, terminate, change job) are only available via SOAP API (Human_Resources, Staffing services). REST API coverage is expanding but incomplete for full lifecycle management.

sdk.notes: No official npm package. SOAP operations can use 'strong-soap' npm module. REST calls use standard HTTP clients.

webhooks.notes: Workday does not natively support webhooks. Polling-based integration is required. Some third-party platforms (e.g., Knit) offer virtual webhooks. Workday PECI (Payroll Effective Change Interface) provides change feeds for payroll providers.

sandbox.notes: Sandbox (implementation) tenants are available to Workday customers. Third-party vendors typically need a partnership agreement with Workday. Workday Community site offers some free developer resources.

needs_verification: Exact OAuth authorize URL format (tenant-specific, varies by data center)
Exact SCIM endpoint path for HCM provisioning
Complete list of REST API scopes (Staffing, Public Data, Tenant Non-Configurable, Recruiting)
Whether REST API supports direct hire/terminate or requires SOAP
